reason: Response B exhibits hallmarks of Claude Opus's distinctive style: philosophical density, abstract conceptual framing ("negative theology"), and a tendency to treat concrete subjects as vehicles for metaphysical reflection rather than pure sensory description. Opus characteristically reaches for unusual intellectual registers and compounds layered clauses into a single sustained argument. Response A, while competent and atmospheric, reads as a more conventional "noir city at night" pastiche — vivid but formulaic in its jazz-and-neon imagery, closer to what a style-mimicking model might produce when prompted to sound literary.
